macro_summary: Thursday's session saw the S&P 500 edge -0.06% lower as the April PCE inflation reading of 3.8%, in line with expectations but at a three-year high, cemented the Fed's hawkish hold. Oil prices jumped 1.8% (WTI $88.62) after fresh U.S. strikes on Iranian boats revived Strait of Hormuz supply fears, though a ceasefire draft remains under discussion. Dell surged 39% afterhours on a raised AI server outlook, signaling relentless hyperscaler demand, while the US10Y yield fell 2.6 bp to 4.45% on the week's oil decline.
commodities_note: WTI rose 0.5% to $88.62 on renewed Hormuz tensions but is still down 8% this week on ceasefire hopes. Gold inched up 0.3% to $4,544/oz, while VIX stayed low at 15.81 — complacent despite geopolitical risks. The US dollar index was flat at 99.11, and the 10-year yield eased to 4.45%, partly reversing recent hawkish pressure.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains hard-blocked on overbought RSI and thin volume. UOB's RSI 67 is the least extended, but all three await the dual NIM relief triggers: US10Y breaking below 4.20% and the October MAS review. Q1 GDP growth of 6% has not translated into bank earnings acceleration, keeping the cluster in CAUTION.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally pressured — dual NIM compression channels active, no relief signal.
singapore.key_development: No catalytic change; US10Y at 4.45% remains above the 4.20% threshold, and MAS policy unchanged until October.

semiconductors.summary: Extreme divergence defines the semis cluster: memory (MU +22% this week, SK Hynix +16%) and AI networking soar on Dell's raised AI server outlook and Nvidia's Taiwan investment, while ASML, TSMC, and AVGO lag by double-digit percentage points over the month. All names are CAUTION due to extension, but the leadership is concentrated in HBM and photonics. The SOXX's +9.5% 5-day gain masks internal fragility; a regime shift may be underway from structural to speculative momentum.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extreme — memory/optics leadership is unsustainable without consolidation.
semiconductors.key_development: Dell raised AI server revenue outlook to $60B, and Nvidia announced $150B/year Taiwan investment, fueling the memory and networking rally.

big_tech.summary: MSFT broke above the 5% extension threshold, moving from HOLD to CAUTION, while AMZN and GOOG remain solidly CAUTION on sustained extension. MSFT's R:R of 2.3:1 is attractive but capped by tight upside resistance at $432. The cluster benefits from AI infrastructure spending, but higher yields and hawkish Fed rhetoric cap multiples.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed but multiple compression risk from rates.
big_tech.key_development: Dell earnings confirmed hyperscaler capex strength, but the bond selloff resumption (10Y still 4.45%) pressures BigTech valuations.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received NVDA's matching $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — AI datacenter co-packaged optics read-through.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA photonics order book accelerates at Computex next week; current 12x forward PE but extreme extension likely.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) — direct AVGO competitor and read-through on hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Reassess after June 4th AVGO earnings for custom ASIC demand signal; a strong report would validate MRVL's revenue growth thesis.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron — Samsung qualification status with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung's HBM4E sample shipment (per Reuters, May 29) leads to NVDA qualification within 2 months; current extreme extension likely blocks.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping despite ongoing US-Iran ceasefire negotiations; only a handful of escorted vessels traversed last week.
US-Iran war drives persistent energy inflation, with April PCE hitting 3.8% YoY, its highest since May 2023, and the Fed signaling rate hikes if oil pass-through persists.
US-China tech confrontation continues, with Taiwan suspecting Nvidia chips smuggled to China via Japan and the MATCH Act targeting ASML exports still active.
ECB rate hike signal for June 11 adds global tightening pressure, diverging from the Fed's hawkish hold but amplifying bond market volatility.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $88.62 is down 17% from its peak but the risk of re-escalation keeps a floor under prices. Tech multiples remain compressed by the 4.45% 10Y, hurting BigTech (AMZN, MSFT) while defense (PLTR) and energy-adjacent names (BE) benefit. Singapore banks face a dual NIM squeeze from elevated US rates and MAS FX policy, leaving DBS, OCBC, UOB in CAUTION.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Fresh US strikes on Iranian boats revived Strait disruption fears, though a ceasefire draft MoU remains under discussion (per CNBC, May 28). April PCE inflation came in at 3.8%, in line with expectations but at a three-year high (per Reuters, May 28).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy no new capital until the VIX breaks above 22 or a clear NFP/CPI catalyst (June 5/10) offers a technical reset; focus on preserving cash for post-earnings entries in semis.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — technical extension blocks entry across 18 of 26 names
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 18 of 26 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, with the semis cluster in extreme divergence. The VIX at 15.81 is complacent given Hormuz and rate risks. The next 7 days bring AVGO earnings, JOLTS, NFP, and CPI — catalysts that could shift the posture.

watch_for_entry.note: Enter $7.23-7.42, stop below $7.23. R:R 2.7:1 to $7.95. Starter position — ADR delisting risk caps sizing. No binary catalyst near-term.
